quotes.text: You can literally choose between health and illness; between a concentration upon the mental more than the physical, or upon the physical more than the mental. Such private decisions affect the genetic heritage of the species. Your intent is all-important -- for you can alter your own genetic messages3 within certain limits. You can cause a cell, or a group of cells, to change their self-image, for example; and again, you do this often -- as you healed yourselves of diseases because of your intent to become well. The intent will be conscious, though the means may not be. Period. In such a case, however, the self-healing qualities of the cells are reinforced, and the self-healing abilities of the species are also strengthened.
quotes.translation: 你确实可以在健康与疾病之间做出选择；可以选择更注重心智而非身体，或更注重身体而非心智。这类私人决定会影响物种的遗传传承。你的意图至关重要——因为你能在特定限度内改变自身的遗传讯息。例如，你能让一个细胞或一组细胞改变其自我形象；而且你经常这样做——就像你因意图康复而治愈自身疾病那样。意图是有意识的，尽管方法可能并非如此。句号。然而在这种情况下，细胞的自我疗愈特质会得到增强，物种的自我疗愈能力也会随之强化。

quotes.text: One animal chasing and killing its prey serves the greater purpose of preserving the balance of nature, whether or not the animal is aware of this -- and again, the animal's intent is not evil. Man consumes ideas. In so doing he contributes to a different kind of balance, of which he is usually unaware. But no man truly acts out of the pure intent to do wrong, or to be vicious. Storms rend the summer sky, sending forth thunder and lightning. Earthquakes may ravage the countryside. You may deeply regret the havoc worked, knowing that neither the storm nor the earthquake is evil. Not only did they have no wrong intent, but the overall condition corrected the earth's balance.
quotes.translation: 动物追逐并杀死猎物，无论它是否意识到，都在服务于维持自然平衡的更高目的——而且再次强调，动物的意图并非邪恶。人类消费思想。在此过程中，他促成了另一种通常不自知的平衡。但没有人真正出于纯粹作恶或行凶的意图而行动。暴风雨撕裂夏空，释放雷电。地震可能蹂躏乡野。你或许深深痛惜所造成的破坏，但深知暴风雨与地震皆非邪恶。它们不仅没有错误意图，而且整体状况修正了地球的平衡。

quotes.text: Disease is not evil, for example. The murderer kills no one, yet if his intent is to do so then he must face the consequences of his intent. Crime after death is not punished. quotes.text: If, however, you pause first and wait a moment, you can begin to glimpse the environment that serves as a stage: the natural landscape of the dream reality. In waking life, if you want to disconnect yourself from an event or place, you try to move away from it in space. In dream reality events occur in a different fashion, and places spring up about you. If you meet with people or events not of your liking, then you must simply move your attention away from them, and they will disappear as far as your experience is concerned. In physical reality you can move fairly freely through space, but you do not travel from one city to another, for example, unless you want to. Intent is invoked. This is so obvious that its significance escapes you: but it is intent that moves you through space, and that is behind all of your physical locomotion. You utilize ships, automobiles, trains, airplanes, because you want to go to another place, and certain vehicles work best under certain conditions.
quotes.translation: 然而，如果你先暂停片刻并等待，你就能开始瞥见作为舞台的环境：梦实相的自然景观。在醒时生活中，若想脱离某个事件或地点，你会试图在空间中远离它。在梦实相中，事件以不同方式发生，地点在你周围涌现。若遇到不喜的人或事，你只需将注意力移开，它们就会从你的经验中消失。在物质实相中，你可以在空间中相当自由地移动，但除非你愿意，否则你不会无缘无故从一座城市旅行到另一座。意图被唤起了。这显而易见，以至于其重要性被你忽略：但正是意图驱动你穿越空间，并存在于所有身体运动背后。你使用船只、汽车、火车、飞机，因为你想去另一个地方，而某些交通工具在特定条件下效果最佳。

quotes.text: Many of my readers are familiar with private meditation, when concentration is focused in one particular area. There are many methods and schools of thought here, but a highly suggestive state of mind results, in which spiritual, mental, and physical goals are sought. It is impossible to meditate without a goal, for that intent is itself a purpose. Unfortunately, many of your public health programs, and commercial statements through the various media, provide you with mass meditations of a most deplorable kind. I refer to those in which the specific symptoms of various diseases are given, in which the individual is further told to examine the body with those symptoms in mind. I also refer to those statements that just as unfortunately specify diseases for which the individual may experience no symptoms of an observable kind, but is cautioned that these disastrous physical events may be happening despite his or her feelings of good health. Here the generalized fears fostered by religious, scientific, and cultural beliefs are often given as blueprints of diseases in which a person can find a specific focus -- the individual can say: "Of course, I feel listless, or panicky, or unsafe since I have such-and-such a disease."
quotes.translation: 我的许多读者熟悉私人冥想，此时注意力集中在某个特定领域。这里有许多方法和思想流派，但会产生一种高度暗示性的心智状态，在其中寻求灵性、心智和身体目标。没有目标就无法冥想，因为那个意图本身就是目的。不幸的是，你们许多公共卫生计划以及通过各种媒体传播的商业声明，提供了最可悲的大众冥想。我指的是那些列出各种疾病具体症状的声明，进一步告诉个体带着这些症状检查身体。我也指那些同样不幸的声明，它们指定个体可能没有明显症状的疾病，但警告说这些灾难性的身体事件可能正在发生，尽管他或她感觉健康良好。在这里，宗教、科学和文化信仰所培养的普遍恐惧常被作为疾病蓝图，个体可以找到特定焦点——可以说："当然，我感到无精打采、恐慌或不安全，因为我患有某某疾病。"

quotes.text: The skin and bone, being physical, are adequate barriers to bound other material, but they have no hold over what is not material. quotes.text: Within him there are methods of perception that allow him to see through the camouflage settings, to see beyond the stage. They are themselves part of the camouflage, but they are like lenses over your natural inner perceptions that force you to "see" an available field of activity as physical matter; and so they can be relied upon only to tell you what is happening in a superficial manner. You can tell the position of the other actors for example, or time by clock, but these physical senses will not tell you that time is itself a camouflage, or that consciousness forms the other actors, or that realities that you cannot see exist over and beyond the physical matter that is so apparent.
quotes.translation: 在他之内，有感知的方法让他看穿伪装设定，看到舞台之外。他不断使用这些内在感官，尽管他作为演员的那部分如此专注于戏剧，以至于这被他忽略了。在很大程度上，物质感官实际上形成了它们似乎只感知到的物质实相。它们本身就是伪装的一部分，但它们就像你自然内在感知上的透镜，迫使你“看到”一个可用的活动场域作为物质；因此，它们只能被依赖来告诉你表面发生的事情。例如，你可以知道其他演员的位置，或通过时钟知道时间，但这些物质感官不会告诉你时间本身就是一种伪装，或意识形成了其他演员，或你看不见的实相存在于如此明显的物质之外。

quotes.text: Often, for instance, you handle probabilities very well, while remaining consciously blind to them because of your concepts. Even then, however, on other levels your unconscious reaction will follow your own conscious intents. You may make a move in physical life, for example, seemingly for one reason. You may also be unconsciously reacting to quite pertinent data regarding the probable actions of others. Because you do not really fully accept the fact that you can so react, you may block this unofficial information on the one hand, even while on the other you take it into consideration. You are far more aware than you realize of the probable future in areas with which you are concerned. This is true on all levels. If your purposes do not involve illness, for instance, and yet if you believe in contagion, you will automatically avoid circumstances that can lead to epidemics. In terms of probabilities that particular kind will not enter your experience.
quotes.translation: 例如，你常常很好地处理可能性，同时由于你的概念而在意识上对它们视而不见。然而，即使如此，在其他层面上，你的无意识反应会跟随你自身的意识意图。例如，你可能会在物质生活中采取一个行动，表面上出于一个原因。你可能也在无意识地对关于他人可能行动的相当相关的数据做出反应。因为你并不真正完全接受你能如此反应的事实，你可能一方面阻止这种非官方信息，另一方面却又将其纳入考虑。你比你意识到的更清楚与你相关的领域的可能未来。这在所有层面上都是如此。例如，如果你的目的不涉及疾病，而你又相信传染，你会自动避免可能导致流行病的情况。在可能性方面，那种特定类型不会进入你的经验。

quotes.text: In your terms, consciousness of self did not develop because of any exterior circumstances in which your species won out, so to speak. In fact, that consciousness of self in any person is dependent upon the constant, miraculous cooperations that exist between the mineral, vegetable, and animal worlds.3 The inner intent always forms any exterior alteration. This applies on any scale you use. Consciousness forms the environment. The environment itself is conscious (forcefully). Atoms and molecules themselves operate in their own fields of probabilities. In their own ways, they "yearn" toward all probable developments. When they form living creatures they become a physical basis for species alteration. The body's adaptability is not simply an adjusting mechanism or quality. The cells have inner capabilities that you have not discovered. They contain within themselves memory of all the "previous" forms they have been a part of.
quotes.translation: 用你们的说法，自我意识的发展并非因为任何外在环境使人类胜出，可以这么说。事实上，任何人的自我意识都依赖于矿物、植物和动物世界之间持续、奇迹般的合作。内在意图总是形成任何外在改变。这适用于你使用的任何尺度。意识形成环境。环境本身是有意识的（有力地）。原子和分子本身在其自身的可能性场域中运作。以它们自己的方式，它们“渴望”所有可能的发展。当它们形成生物时，它们成为物种改变的物质基础。身体的适应性不仅仅是一种调整机制或品质。细胞具有你尚未发现的内在能力。它们自身包含了对它们曾参与的所有“先前”形式的记忆。

quotes.text: When you are considering the future in your terms, constructive achievements are as realistic as destructive ones. In those terms, each year of man's existence in fact justifies a more optimistic rather than pessimistic view. You cannot place man's good intent outside of the physical context, for outside of that context you do not have the creature that you know. You cannot say that nature is good, but spawned man, which is a cancer upon it, for nature would have better sense. You cannot say, either, that Nature -- with a capital N -- will destroy man if he offends her, or that Nature -- with a capital N -- has little use for its own species, but only wants to promote Life -- with a capital L -- for Nature is within each member of each species; and without each member of each species, Nature -- with a capital or a small N -- would be nonexistent.
quotes.translation: 当你们以自身的时间观念考虑未来时，建设性的成就与破坏性的成就同样真实。就那些时间观念而言，人类存在的每一年实际上都证明更乐观而非悲观的观点是合理的。你不能将人类的善意置于物质脉络之外，因为在那脉络之外，你便没有你所认知的这个生物。你不能说自然是善的，却孕育了人类这个寄生其上的毒瘤，因为自然会有更明智的判断。你也不能说自然——大写N——若人类冒犯它便会毁灭人类，或者说自然——大写N——对其自身物种几乎毫无用处，只想促进生命——大写L——因为自然存在于每个物种的每个成员之内；没有每个物种的每个成员，自然——无论大写还是小写——都将不复存在。

quotes.text: At cellular levels the world exists with a kind of social interchange, in which the birth and death of cells are known to all others, and in which the death of a frog and a star gain equal weight. But at your level of activity your thoughts, feelings, and intents, however private, form part of the inner environment of communication. This inner environment is as pertinent and vital to the species' well-being as is the physical one. It represents the psychic, mass bank of potential, even as the planet provides a physical bank of potential. When there is an earthquake in another area of the world, the land mass in your own country is in one way or another affected. When there are psychic earthquakes in other areas of the world, then you are also affected, and usually to the same degree.
quotes.translation: 在细胞层面，世界以一种社会性交流的方式存在，其中细胞的生与死为所有其他细胞所知，青蛙的死亡与星辰的死亡具有同等分量。但在你们的活动层面，你们的思想、情感和意图，无论多么私密，都构成了内在沟通环境的一部分。这个内在环境对物种福祉的重要性与物质环境同样关键且不可或缺。它代表了灵性的、集体的潜能库，正如行星提供了物质的潜能库。当世界另一地区发生地震时，你们自己国家的大地在某种程度上也会受到影响。当世界其他地区发生灵性地震时，你们同样会受到影响，而且程度通常相同。

quotes.text: Your body is the physical mirror of your psychological state (quietly intent). It is powered by the energy of the universe. It actually springs into being in each moment. Your mind and your body come from the same source, from universal energy. You are powered with vitality. You must seek meaning in your lives. When you lose the sense of life's meaning, for whatever reason, this is reflected in your body. (Pause.) It is very difficult to separate all of this from the many connotations placed about disease, and I do not want the material to be misread (still intently). Cancer, for example, has become the symbol for the body's vulnerability, in current years -- the proof of man's susceptibility to the body. It is a disease that people have when they want to die -- when they are ashamed to admit that they want to die, because death seems to fly against sane behavior. If the species struggles to survive, then how can individuals want to die?
quotes.translation: 你们的身体是你们心理状态的物质镜子（安静而专注）。它由宇宙的能量驱动。它实际上在每一刻都瞬间生成。你们的心灵和身体来自同一个源头，来自宇宙能量。你们被赋予生命力。你们必须在生活中寻求意义。当你们因任何原因失去生命意义感时，这就会反映在你们的身体上。（停顿。）很难将这一切与围绕疾病的众多含义区分开来，我不希望这些材料被误解（依然专注地）。例如，在当今时代，癌症已成为身体脆弱性的象征——证明人类易受身体影响。这是一种人们在想要死亡时患上的疾病——当他们羞于承认自己想要死亡，因为死亡似乎违背了理智的行为。如果物种挣扎求生，那么个体又怎能想要死亡呢？

quotes.text: As inner energy forms more complicated gestalts through the processes that I have earlier explained, then inner energy continues its communication with the physical matter that it creates about it. When inner energy desires to construct a more complicated gestalt, then it must telepathically communicate this intent and purpose through the matter which it collects about itself, forming a more complicated inner telepathic pattern first, that can then be filled in with physical matter. The manners in which telepathy operates are difficult to explain because of the divergence of definition held by the ego and by the inner self of any given individual. To the human being it must appear that telepathy is a mental or psychic communication occurring between two or more entirely separate selves. The ego, you see, has one definition for the self and its own idea of the limitations of itself.
quotes.translation: 当内在能量通过我之前解释的过程形成更复杂的完形时，内在能量便继续与其所创造的周围物质进行沟通。当内在能量想要构建一个更复杂的完形时，它必须通过其周围收集的物质以心电感应方式传达这一意图和目的，首先形成一个更复杂的内在心电感应模式，然后才能用物质来填充。心电感应运作的方式很难解释，因为任何特定个体的自我和内在自我对定义持有分歧。对人类而言，心电感应必定表现为两个或更多完全独立的自我之间发生的精神或灵性沟通。你看，自我对自我有自身的定义，并对自身的局限性有自身的看法。

quotes.text: You spoke earlier of tragedies, and I do not understand you. Great work stands alone. It is a triumph of the human spirit, and it always grows out of experience. As an egotistical creature, alone, you would not choose the experience, even though great work resulted from it. (Smile, and intent delivery.) Learning involves your whole being. Great work is a result of great learning. It is not the result of seeing easy answers scribbled magically upon a blackboard. The flowers thrust themselves up through soil, but they hardly consider the soil or thrusting a tragedy, nor resent the time spent in the frozen earth, for they realize the frozen earth is a condition of their blossoming--a challenge that is an aid, not a hindrance. The light does not resent the darkness which it illuminates, you see? Your inner self is aware of this.
quotes.translation: 你早些时候谈到悲剧，我不理解你。伟大的作品独立存在。它是人类精神的胜利，并且总是源于经验。作为一个孤立的、自我中心的生物，你不会选择那种经验，即使伟大的作品由此而生。（微笑，专注地传达。）学习涉及你的整个存在。伟大的作品是伟大学习的结果。它不是看到轻松答案神奇地潦草写在黑板上的结果。花朵奋力破土而出，但它们几乎不认为土壤或破土是悲剧，也不怨恨在冻土中度过的时光，因为它们意识到冻土是其绽放的条件——一种助益而非阻碍的挑战。光明不会怨恨它所照亮的黑暗，你明白吗？你的内在自我深知这一点。

quotes.text: (From the 775th session for May 10, 1976:) Bits of your consciousnesses,34 Joseph and Ruburt, go out through these books. I am not speaking symbolically. Those portions will mix with the consciousness of others. Portions of your intent and purpose become theirs. My own psychological reality is not particleized. My identity includes the identities of many others, and they each operate in their own fashion. In those terms I am a wave formation. More specifically, however, and to a lesser degree, each physical person operates partially as a particleized being, and partially in terms of a wave.35 But identity, being itself inviolate, is on the other hand everchanging -- and there is, in the larger framework of reality, no contradiction.

quotes.text: Much is not understood in your interpretations. In that world men knew that nature was balanced. Both animals and men must die. And when they slayed other animals themselves and ate the heart, for example, it was not only to obtain the animals' "stout hearts," or fearlessness; but also the intent was to preserve those characteristics so that through men's experiences each animal would continue to live to some extent.
quotes.translation: 你们的解读中有许多未被理解之处。在那个世界，人类知道自然是平衡的。动物和人类都必须死亡。如果有人被动物捕获并吃掉（这种情况时有发生），他的同伴并不会怨恨那只动物——至少在最深层的意义上不会。而当他们自己猎杀其他动物并吃掉心脏时，不仅是为了获取动物的"勇敢之心"或无畏精神；其意图也是为了保存那些特质，以便通过人类的经验，每种动物都能在一定程度上继续存活。

quotes.text: The physical reality into which you are born is not nearly as solid or predetermined or definite as it appears to be. Instead there is a field of rich interaction. Your consciousness must be focused at one particular range of frequencies before it can even perceive matter, much less solidity. In sleep your consciousness fluctuates between ranges of intensities, literally flowing into and out of the physical-matter grouping, and forming from more plastic "pre-matter" (with a hyphen) stages, the final shape that matter will take in your world. The same applies to events, where some will be crystallized in physical terms and others will not. The deep portions of your own being are aware of those purposes and intents that are uniquely yours. Unconsciously, then, you have within you what you might think of as a set of blueprints for the particular kind of physical reality you want to materialize. You are the architect.
quotes.translation: 你们所出生的物质实相远非表面所见那般坚固、预定或确定。相反，它是一片丰饶的互动场域。你们的意识必须聚焦于特定频率范围，才能感知物质——更遑论坚固性。睡眠时，你们的意识在强度区间波动，实质上流动进出物质群组，并从更具可塑性的"前物质"（带连字符）阶段，塑造出物质在你们世界中的最终形态。事件亦然——有些会结晶为物质形式，有些则不会。你们存在的深层部分知晓那些独属于你们的目的与意图。因此，在无意识层面，你们内在拥有可视为蓝图集的东西——那是你们想要物质化的特定物质实相。你们就是建筑师。

quotes.text: In the dream state and in certain other levels of reality, ideas and their symbols are immediately experienced. There is no time lag, then, between a feeling and its "exteriorized" condition. It is automatically experienced in whatever form is familiar and natural to the one who holds it. The psyche is presented with its own concepts, which are instantly reflected in dream situations and other events that will be explained shortly. If you dream of or yearn for a new house in physical life, for instance, it may take some time before that ideal is realized, even though such a strong intent will most certainly bring about its physical fulfillment. The same desire in the dreaming state, however, may lead to the instant creation of such a house as far as your dream experience is concerned. Again, there is no time lag there between desire and its materialization.
quotes.translation: 在梦境状态及其他某些实相层面中，观念及其象征会立即被体验。因此，感觉与其"外显"状态之间没有时间差。它会自动以持有者熟悉且自然的形式被体验。心灵会呈现自身概念，这些概念会立即反映在梦境情境及稍后将解释的其他事件中。例如，若你在物质生活中梦见或渴望一栋新房子，即使如此强烈的意图必定会带来物质层面的实现，但理想成真仍需时日。然而就梦境体验而言，同样的渴望可能瞬间创造出这样一栋房子。再次强调，在欲望与其物质化之间没有时间差。

quotes.text: The child knew "that it came from somewhere else" -- not by chance but by design. The child knew that in one way or another its most intimate thoughts, dreams, and gestures were as connected with the natural world as blades of grass are to a field. The child knew it was a unique and utterly original event or being that on the one hand was its own focus, and that on the other hand belonged to its own time and season. In fact, children let little escape them, so that, again, they experiment constantly in an effort to discover not only the effect of their thoughts and intents and wishes upon others, but the degree to which others influence their own behavior. To that extent, they deal rather directly with probabilities in a way quite foreign to adult behavior.
quotes.translation: 孩子知道"它来自别处"——并非偶然而是设计。孩子知道其最私密的思绪、梦境与姿态，在某种程度上与自然世界的关联，如同草叶之于原野。孩子知道自己是独特且完全原创的事件或存在，一方面拥有自身焦点，另一方面属于特定时空。事实上，孩子几乎不会遗漏任何事物，因此他们不断实验，不仅试图发现自身思想、意图和愿望对他人的影响，也探索他人影响自身行为的程度。就此而言，他们以与成人行为截然不同的方式相当直接地处理或然性。

quotes.text: Obviously there are objects of all sizes, durability, and weight. There are private objects and public ones. There are also "vast psychological objects," then, sweeping mass events, for example, in which whole countries might be involved. There are also mass natural events of varying degrees, as say, the flooding of large areas. Such events involve psychological configurations on the part of all those involved, so that the inner individual patterns of those lives touched by each such event have in one way or another a common purpose that at the same time serves the overall reality on a natural planetary basis. In order to endure, the planet itself must be involved in constant change and instability. I know it is difficult to comprehend, but every object that you perceive -- grass or rock or stone -- even ocean waves or clouds -- any physical phenomenon -- has its own invisible consciousness, its own intent and emotional coloration. Each is also endowed with patterns toward growth and fulfillment -- not at the expense of the rest of nature, but to the contrary, so that every other element of nature may also be completed (all with much emphasis).
quotes.translation: 显然存在各种尺寸、耐久度和重量的物体。有私人物品与公共物品。还有"庞大的心理客体"，例如席卷整个国家的大规模群体事件。也存在不同程度的自然群体事件，如大面积洪水。这类事件涉及所有参与者的心理构型，因此每个事件所触及的生命的内在个体模式，都以某种方式具有共同目的，同时服务于行星层面的整体实相。为了存续，行星本身必须参与持续变化与不稳定。我知道这难以理解，但你们感知的每个物体——草叶、岩石、石块，甚至海浪或云朵——任何物理现象——都有其无形意识、自身意图与情感色彩。每个物体还被赋予成长与完成的模式——并非以牺牲自然其他部分为代价，恰恰相反，是为了让自然界的每个其他元素也能圆满（着重强调）。

quotes.text: There is a correlation here with something Ruburt said in [ESP] class last evening. He said that writing can be, first, a method of standing apart from life to some extent -- in order to capture life, and preserve the unutterable uniqueness of any given day. But, he said, you can then discover that the writing itself becomes the day's experience. You are then "lost" in the writing as much as you feared being lost in normal living, with no way to step aside and view the experience. My addition, now, to those remarks is this: You would need the creation then of another "self," who stood aside from the writing self in order to preserve the original intent.
quotes.translation: 这与鲁柏昨晚在[ESP]课上的言论存在关联。他说写作首先可以是某种程度上的抽离生活——为了捕捉生活，保存任何特定日子难以言喻的独特性。但他又说，随后你会发现写作本身变成了那天的体验。你"迷失"在写作中的程度，与你曾恐惧在正常生活中迷失的程度相当，同样无法抽身旁观体验。现在我要补充的是：那时你需要创造另一个"自我"，从写作自我中抽离出来，以保存最初的意图。

source_raw.title: Every so often I've thought of averaging Jane's dictation time for Seth Speaks and Personal Reality in the same way, but haven't done so. I'm somewhat puzzled to note, however, that her very short working times for the Seth books seem to be either ignored or taken for granted by practically everyone -- or, perhaps, those factors just aren't understood in terms of ordinary linear time. Maybe I'm alone in my interest here, for even Jane doesn't express any great curiosity about the time she has invested in the Seth material; she just delivers it. But given her abilities, I think her speed of production is a close physical approach to, or translation of, Seth's idea that basically all exists at once -- that really there is no time, and that the Seth books, for example, are "there" to be had in final form for just the tuning in. (In Section 3 of this volume, Note 2 for Session 692 contains information on another way by which we can move closer to Seth's idea of simultaneity from our physical reality, but that method grows out of material not discussed here.)

quotes.text: The experiences at these various levels may be interpreted symbolically. They may appear in the form of fantasy, fiction, or art work, without the conscious self realizing their origin. quotes.text: There are also what I will call body dreams. No consciousness, to whatever degree, is fully manifested in matter. quotes.text: I also believe, however, that generally speaking science still views our genetic systems in mechanical, deterministic, and reductionistic terms, and will continue to do so for a long time: So that evidence is being accumulated to support that overall view that at this time science has no need to seek for other, larger, and more unsettling frames of reference encompassing consciousness, intent, and genetics. Indeed, I seldom see consciousness mentioned in connection with genetics, except as its quality may relate to genetic "defects" like mental retardation, say.
quotes.translation: 但我同样认为，总体而言科学界仍以机械论、决定论和还原论的视角看待基因系统，且这种状况将持续很久：因此积累的证据都在支持这种整体观点，使得科学目前无需寻求其他更宏大、更令人不安的参照系——那些涵盖意识、意图与遗传学的框架。事实上，我极少看到基因学研究提及意识，除非其特质与智力障碍等基因"缺陷"相关。

quotes.text: Men in those times protected themselves against storms, and yet in the same way they did not begrudge the storm its victims. They simply changed the alliances of their consciousnesses from the identification of self-within-the-flesh to self-within-the-storm. Man's and nature's intents were largely the same, and understood as such. Man did not fear the elements in those early times, as is now supposed.
quotes.translation: 古人会抵御风暴，却不会为风暴造成的伤亡而怨怼。他们只是将意识联盟从"肉身中的自我"转变为"风暴中的自我"。人类与自然的意图大体相同，且被如此理解。古人并不像现代人想象的那样畏惧自然元素。

source_raw.title: Regardless of your histories, those early men and women were quite healthy. They had strong teeth and bones. quotes.text: Again, such times are closely bound with reincarnational intents that direct the genetic triggering, and that meet in the culture the further stimulus that may be required. source_raw.title: Here in Dreams Seth uses the uncertainty principle as an analogy (and an excellent one), meaning that as the positions and motions of elementary particles, say, cannot be simultaneously measured precisely, so our genetic qualities and their motions can not always be specifically determined. In Dreams he's already said (in Session 909) that the human species has an "amazing interplay between genetic preciseness and genetic freedom," and (in Session 910) that "your genetic structure reacts to each thought that you have, to the state of your emotions, to your psychological climate." Choices and probabilities apply. Thus do we avoid genetic rigidity.

quotes.text: He heard tomorrow's weather report (yesterday), groaned, thought of a very uncomfortable 90-degree temperature tomorrow [and] imagined himself miserable with the heat. Indeed, he began to feel warmer. In a flash he remembered previous days of discomfort, and in the next moment he projected those into the weekend. He felt trapped. Midway through this process he tried to catch himself, but he believed that his body could not handle the heat -- and that belief outweighed his intent to change his thoughts, so they kept returning for perhaps ten minutes.
quotes.translation: 他（昨天）听到明日天气预报后呻吟起来，想到明天90华氏度的酷热就觉得自己会被热得苦不堪言。果然，他开始感到燥热。瞬间他忆起往日的酷暑难耐，下一秒便将这种感受投射到整个周末。他感到被困住了。过程中他曾试图悬崖勒马，但他深信自己的身体无法承受高温——这个信念压倒了改变想法的意图，于是那些念头持续回返了约十分钟。

quotes.text: The health and psychic condition of an individual is not primarily determined by the ego, however. It is only when the ego is allowed too much power that the individual is deprived of much of the inner vitality of the whole self. For the ego is acquainted with only its role. It can find refreshment only within the limitations of the reality which it was formed to meet; and when it looks about with the best of intentions and sees disasters and terrors, it does not know that these others also play their roles, and that the roles are temporary.
quotes.translation: 然而，个体的健康与心灵状态并非主要由自我决定。只有当自我被赋予过多权力时，个体才会被剥夺整体自性的内在活力。因为自我只熟悉自己的角色，只能在它被塑造来应对的现实限制中找到更新；当它怀着最善意的意图环顾四周，看到灾难与恐惧时，它不知道这些角色也各有其位，且角色都是暂时的。

quotes.text: The idea should be an abstract philosophical concept. This at least, can be done by initial conscious intent. There must always be a balance between necessary conditioning, ritual and habit, and spontaneity, freedom, and what we will call instantaneous psychic relaxations.

quotes.text: Your material has not been coming through clearly for the reasons given. This does not mean that it will not improve, for it will if you persist. quotes.text: There is no separate field that combines all of that information, or applies the facts of one discipline to the facts of another discipline, so overall, science, with its brand of rational thought, can offer no even, suggestive, hypothetical, comprehensive ideas of what reality is. It seems that each individual is in effect isolated in certain vital regards -- given, say, a genetic heritage and a certain amount of unspecified energy with which to run the body's machinery (intently). Intent, purpose, or desire do not apply in that picture.
quotes.translation: 没有一个独立的领域能整合所有这些信息，或将一门学科的事实应用于另一门学科的事实。因此，总体而言，科学以其理性思维的标签，无法提供任何关于实相本质的、哪怕是暗示性的、假设性的、全面的概念。似乎每个个体在某些关键方面实际上都是孤立的——比如说，被赋予一份遗传遗产和一定量的未指定能量，用以（专注地）运行身体的机制。意图、目的或欲望在那个图景中并不适用。

quotes.text: There are many later-appended references in the Bible, such as the fig tree story, in which nature is played down. Christ's "father" was, however, the God who was indeed aware of every sparrow that fell, who knew of every creature's existence, whatever its species or kind. The story of the shepherds and flocks comes much closer to Christ's intent, where each creature looked out for the others.
quotes.translation: 圣经中有许多后来附加的引用，例如无花果树的故事，其中贬低了自然。然而，基督的“天父”确实是那位知晓每一只麻雀坠落、知晓每一个造物存在的上帝，无论其物种或种类。牧羊人与羊群的故事更接近基督的意图，其中每个造物都彼此照看。

quotes.text: You live in a physical community, but you live first of all in a community of thoughts and feelings. These trigger your physical actions. They directly affect the behavior of your body. quotes.text: An individual or a self also cannot hide from others his own basic intent. It is his and yet, though he possesses it, he still cannot prevent others from sensing it. Along these lines there is much to be said in that many intangibles, considered most secret by the self, do not remain within the self. No skin or bones or skeletal cage can keep the thought of the self from going outward.
quotes.translation: 个体或自我也无法向他人隐藏自己的基本意图。这意图既属于他，即便他拥有它，仍无法阻止他人感知。沿着这条思路，有许多可说的：那些被自我视为最隐秘的无形之物，并不会停留在自我之内。没有皮肤、骨骼或骨架能阻止自我的思想向外发散。

quotes.text: At certain levels these intents of man and nature may merge. I am speaking in very simple terms now, and yet those involved in a flood, for example, want the past washed away, or want to be flooded by bursts of vital emotions such as disasters often bring. They want to feel a renewed sense of nature's power, and often, though devastated, they use the experience to start a new life.
quotes.translation: 在某些层面，人类与自然的意图可能融合。我现在用极简单的语言说明：例如，卷入洪水的人，往往希望过去被冲刷殆尽，或渴望被灾难常带来的强烈情感洪流所淹没。他们想重新感受自然的力量，即便遭受重创，也常借此经历开启新生。

quotes.text: Events are processed in dreams, put in the necessary perspective, sorted and arranged. This is done when the conscious mind is separated from direct involvement with physical events. Dreams serve to dull the impact of the day's events just past, while the meaning of those activities sifts through the various levels of the personality, settling into compartments of intent and belief. Often the true impact of an event does not occur until it has been interpreted or reexperienced through a dream.
quotes.translation: 事件在梦境中被处理，置于必要视角，分类整理。当意识心智脱离与物理事件的直接纠缠时，这项工作便得以进行。梦境的作用是缓冲刚过去白天的冲击，同时让那些活动的意义渗透人格各层面，归入意图与信念的隔间。往往事件的真正冲击力，要等到通过梦境被诠释或重新体验后才会显现。

quotes.text: Actually the three sets of events could easily occur to the three people at once, and if no normal communication happened no one would be the wiser. In dreams you work with the kind of intensities involved, exploring multitudinous significances. These are like charged emotional patterns, formed of your own highly personal emotions and intents.

quotes.text: The action and counteraction is the time trigger. To you this will be almost unbelievable, but on some other planes motion is simultaneous and time unknown. To me time can be manipulated, used at leisure and examined. To me your time is a vehicle, one of the several vehicles by which I can enter your awareness. It is therefore still a reality of some kind to me. Otherwise I could not utilize it in any manner whatsoever. As an example of my good intentions I will end this session.
quotes.translation: 作用与反作用就是时间触发器。这对你而言几乎难以置信，但在某些其他层面上，运动是同时发生的，时间并不存在。对我而言，时间可以被操控、随意使用并加以检视。对我而言，你们的时间是一种载具，是我能进入你们意识的几种载具之一。因此它对我而言仍是某种实相。否则我根本无法以任何方式利用它。作为我善意的例证，我将结束这次课程。

quotes.text: You are growing in consciousness; therefore using it expands its capabilities. It is not a thing, but an attribute and characteristic. source_raw.title: Here in Dreams Seth uses the uncertainty principle as an analogy (and an excellent one), meaning that as the positions and motions of elementary particles, say, cannot be simultaneously measured precisely, so our genetic qualities and their motions can not always be specifically determined. In Dreams he's already said (in Session 909) that the human species has an "amazing interplay between genetic preciseness and genetic freedom," and (in Session 910) that "your genetic structure reacts to each thought that you have, to the state of your emotions, to your psychological climate." Choices and probabilities apply. Thus do we avoid genetic rigidity.
source_raw.chapter_title: CHAPTER 7

quotes.text: Through such behavior the overall value fulfillment purposes and intents of the species are kept in focus, and those necessary requirements then planted in whatever space or time [is] required. Again, free will still operates in all such ventures.
quotes.translation: 通过此类行为，物种的整体价值完成目的与意图得以保持焦点，那些必要的要求随后被植入所需的空间或时间。再次强调，自由意志在所有此类冒险中依然运作。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

source_raw.title: Jane doesn't agree with my doubts. As always, she's been letting me put this book together the way I think best--and inevitably that way has followed how we've been trying to understand our joint long-term situation. She innocently accepts my labors as they come out. And that trust always reflects, I'm sure, Seth's own larger view of reality, as I just quoted him from Session 915. Our challenges echo throughout all of our probable realities simultaneously, and through all of them together the largest picture of Jane and myself is presented. In this probable reality we work with what we can pick up from that great whole. We keep trying to learn to ask better questions.
source_raw.chapter_title: CHAPTER 9

quotes.text: Science often says that nature cares little for the individual, only for the species, so then you must often see yourselves as victims in a larger struggle for survival, in which your own intents do not carry even the puniest sway.
quotes.translation: 科学常说自然很少关心个体，只关心物种，因此你们常将自己视为生存大搏斗中的受害者，在其中你们的意图连最微小的影响力都发挥不了。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: eth's statement at the end of dictation for the 815th session, "I will try to begin work on our book in a more predictable fashion," reflected his good intent, but things didn't turn out that way. Jane and I let the holiday season intervene to some extent, and Session 816, which came through the day after Christmas, didn't concern book work at all. Then, starting in early January, we held eight sessions having to do with questions we'd put off dealing with for a long time; some were personal and some were not. We stayed with our Monday-Saturday evening session routine, though, and kept busy with all of our other projects.1
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: These therefore for all intents and purposes close ranks, encompassing the three systems and accepting no further additions at this given time. As they evolve they may attract other energy forms, and build extensions to the system.
